title: Explore live Claude TUI attach for tail view
description:
The current tail view (t key in detail view) shows read-only output lines from stream-json parsing. User wants to explore putting the user directly into the live Claude Code TUI so they can observe and interact with the underlying claude instance.

Two options to explore:

**Option A: --resume for attach/detach**
- Keep -p mode for the orchestrator (structured I/O for parsing)
- When user presses t, suspend bubbletea and exec 'claude --resume <session-id>' interactively
- User gets the full Claude Code TUI for that session
- When they quit claude, bubbletea resumes
- Tradeoff: resuming starts a new turn — changes happen outside orchestrator awareness
- Session ID is already available from stream-json result events

**Option B: PTY-based live view**
- Run claude interactively (no -p) inside a PTY (e.g. creack/pty)
- Orchestrator feeds prompts via PTY stdin, reads terminal output
- User 'attaches' to see the real TUI (like tmux attach)
- Tradeoff: lose structured JSON parsing — would need to parse terminal escape codes or find another way to track orchestration state

**Key files:**
- internal/runtime/claude/claude.go (runStreaming - currently uses -p --verbose --output-format stream-json)
- internal/runtime/claude/response.go (event types, session_id captured in result event)
- internal/loop/loop.go (orchestrator loop, OnOutput wiring)
- internal/ui/tail.go (current tail view rendering)
- internal/ui/app.go (bubbletea app, handles t key)

**Prior context:**
- streams-u0x (closed) fixed the stream-json parsing (added --verbose, --include-partial-messages, stream_event handler)
- The current tail view works for showing buffered output lines but is not a live-tail and has no user input
- Commit cfd7568 has the runtime parsing fix
close_reason: Exploration complete. Option A (--resume via tea.ExecProcess) selected. Option B (PTY) deferred — too complex, loses structured JSON. Implementation filed as streams-4k6 -> streams-4ly -> streams-lwp. Plan at docs/plans/valiant-gathering-fern.md

title: Clarify step beads vs review beads in the coding phase
description: The decompose phase creates step beads as children of the parent. The coding review also creates children of the parent. When the implement agent runs bd show --children, how does it distinguish unfinished steps from review feedback? Temporal ordering works in the happy path but breaks if the first pass doesn't close all steps. Proposed resolution: the implement prompt just says 'address all open children' regardless of origin — the agent doesn't need to distinguish them. Validate this approach and document the decision.
close_reason: Resolved: unified the two coding implement prompts (first-pass vs refinement) into a single prompt. The agent determines commit strategy from child issue content — step beads get regular commits, review feedback gets fixup commits. No need for the loop to track pass type.

title: Define snapshot content and what gets stored from CLI output
description: The Snapshot struct has Summary and Review string fields. But Claude CLI JSON output for a full session can be enormous (every tool call, file read, etc). Need to decide what goes into Summary. Options: (1) full CLI JSON response — too large, (2) just final text output — loses tool call detail, (3) structured summary extracted from JSON by the Go loop. This affects persistence size, TUI display capabilities, and debugging. Also: should the Snapshot record which macro-phase it belongs to? Currently it only has Iteration but no phase context.
design:
## Snapshot Content Design

### Snapshot struct

```go
type Snapshot struct {
    Phase       string
    Iteration   int
    Summary     string       // CLI result text (final text output from claude -p --output-format json)
    Review      string       // reviewer's text output
    GateResults []GateResult
    CostUSD     float64
    DiffStat    string       // git diff --stat output (coding phase only)
    CommitSHAs  []string     // commits made this iteration (coding phase only)
    BeadsClosed []string     // bead IDs closed by implement step
    BeadsOpened []string     // bead IDs opened by review step
    Timestamp   time.Time
}
```

### Key decisions

- **Summary source**: Use the `result` field from `--output-format json`. Claude naturally summarizes its work at the end of each invocation, so this is compact and informative without storing the full tool-call trace.
- **No full diffs stored**: Snapshots store commit SHAs and diffstat only. Full diffs are rendered live via `git show <sha>` when the user drills down. Keeps snapshots small.
- **No full CLI JSON**: The complete JSON output (every tool call, file read, etc.) is discarded. The `result` text is sufficient for the "what happened" view.
- **Phase field added**: Each snapshot records which macro-phase produced it, enabling the iteration list to show "Plan 1, Plan 2, Decompose 1, Coding 1..." across the full stream history.
- **Beads delta**: Track IDs opened/closed per iteration for quick "work done / work remaining" signal.

### TUI layout

**Dashboard view** — one row per stream, showing: name, current phase, iteration count. Minimal.

**Detail view** — two-pane layout:
- Left: vertical list of all snapshots across all phases (e.g., "Plan 1", "Plan 2", "Decompose 1", "Coding 1"...). v1 highlights the latest; future adds j/k traversal.
- Right: selected snapshot's details (summary, review, diffstat, gates).
- Keybind `d` (future) could run `git show` on commit SHAs for full diff drill-down.

### What's NOT in v1
- Storing full diffs in snapshots
- Storing full CLI JSON trace
- Plan-phase diffs of plan.md (just read the current file)
- Navigating previous snapshots (v1 shows latest only)
close_reason: Design decided: snapshots store CLI result text + commit SHAs + diffstat + beads delta. No full diffs or CLI JSON. TUI uses two-pane layout in detail view.

title: Session ID collision after attach+restart: 'already in use' error
description: After attaching to a stream and then restarting it, the loop fails with 'Session ID <uuid> is already in use'. The interactive claude --resume session may still be holding the session lock when the loop restarts, or Claude CLI considers a previously-resumed session as permanently claimed. The fix in Start() to call SetSessionID(newUUID()) should address this, but needs verification. If the issue persists, we may need to add a delay or wait for the --resume process to fully release the session.
close_reason: Same SIGINT fix resolves session lock issue. Combined with the existing newUUID() in Start(), the old session is released gracefully and the new session uses a fresh ID

title: Discussion: plan phase convergence logic — should iterate until reviewer files nothing
description: Current convergence check: openChildrenAfter <= openChildrenBefore. Question from Zach: if the reviewer files 5 beads and the next iteration closes all 5, does that count as convergence? Currently YES — because after implement closes them all, openChildrenAfter(impl)=0, then if review files 0 new issues, openChildrenAfter(review)=0 <= 0. But if review files new issues after seeing the revisions, it does NOT converge that iteration. Zach's preference: the phase should keep iterating until the review agent has nothing left to file. Need to revisit whether the current logic already achieves this or if the convergence condition needs adjustment.
close_reason: Analyzed convergence logic — current condition already achieves desired behavior (iterate until reviewer files nothing). Renamed OpenChildrenBefore/OpenChildrenAfter to OpenBeforeReview/OpenAfterReview for clarity.

title: Design pairing mode UX and loop integration
description: ModePairing exists as an enum value but is never consulted. Questions to resolve: (1) How does the user select pairing mode at stream creation? Add a mode toggle to the new-stream overlay, or a separate key binding? (2) Architecture says pairing forces TransitionMode to always return pause — where does this check live: in the loop, in each phase, or as a wrapper? (3) What does the TUI experience look like in pairing mode? Does the user see iteration output and explicitly approve each one? (4) Should pairing mode allow inline editing of the implement prompt before each iteration?
design:
## Pairing Mode Design

### Q1: How does the user select pairing mode?

**Mode toggle in the new-stream overlay.** Add a second line showing the current mode (default: Autonomous). The user presses Tab to toggle between Autonomous and Pairing before pressing Enter to create. The overlay passes the mode to orchestrator.Create().

**Mode switching mid-stream.** Add an `m` key binding on both dashboard and detail views. Pressing `m` toggles the selected stream's mode between Autonomous and Pairing. If the stream is running and switches to Pairing, the current iteration finishes normally, then the loop pauses before starting the next one. If switching to Autonomous while paused, the user still presses `s` to resume — mode switching doesn't auto-start.

Add a GetMode()/SetMode() accessor pair to Stream (follows existing pattern).

### Q2: Where does the pause-on-pairing check live?

**In the loop, at two points:**

1. **After convergence (existing TransitionMode check).** The loop already checks `phase.TransitionMode()` to decide auto-advance vs pause. Add a mode check: if `stream.Mode == ModePairing`, force `TransitionPause` regardless of what the phase returns. This is what ARCHITECTURE.md specifies.

2. **After StepGuidance, before continuing the next iteration.** This is the new behavior: in pairing mode, the loop pauses after EVERY iteration — not just at convergence. The user reviews each snapshot and explicitly resumes with `s`. Implementation: after draining guidance and incrementing the iteration counter, check `stream.GetMode() == ModePairing`. If true, set StatusPaused and return. The user resumes with `s`, which re-enters the loop for the next iteration.

The check lives in the loop (loop.go), not in each phase or as a wrapper. Phases don't need to know about pairing mode — they provide TransitionMode() as a preference, and the loop overrides it when pairing.

### Q3: What does the TUI look like in pairing mode?

**Dashboard:** Show mode indicator next to the stream status. Autonomous streams show nothing extra (it's the default). Pairing streams show a `[Pair]` badge. When paused-in-pairing (normal state between iterations), the status shows `Paused` as usual — the `[Pair]` badge distinguishes it from a convergence pause.

**Detail view:** Same as autonomous — shows the snapshot list and selected snapshot details. The key difference is behavioral: in pairing mode the stream pauses after each iteration, so the user naturally reviews snapshots one at a time.

**Workflow:** The user creates a stream in pairing mode. They press `s` to start the first iteration. The agent runs one implement-review cycle, produces a snapshot, and pauses. The user inspects the snapshot, optionally adds guidance with `g`, then presses `s` to run the next iteration. When satisfied, they press `m` to switch to autonomous and `s` to let it run freely.

**Help bar:** When the selected stream is in pairing mode, add `m: autonomous` to the help hints. When in autonomous mode, show `m: pairing`.

### Q4: Should pairing mode allow inline editing of the implement prompt?

**No, not in v1.** The guidance mechanism already serves this purpose — the user submits free-text direction via `g` that gets injected into the next implement prompt. Adding a prompt editor would duplicate this and add significant UI complexity (multi-line editor with pre-populated prompt text, cursor navigation, etc). If the user wants to steer the agent, they use guidance. If they want to change the fundamental phase behavior, they'd edit the phase prompts (future configurability).

### Implementation scope

Changes needed:
- **stream.go**: Add GetMode()/SetMode() accessors
- **loop.go**: Add pairing-mode pause check after StepGuidance; override TransitionMode on convergence when pairing
- **orchestrator.go**: Accept mode parameter in Create(); add ToggleMode() method
- **app.go**: Wire `m` key binding to orchestrator.ToggleMode(); pass mode from new-stream overlay
- **dashboard.go**: Show [Pair] badge; update help bar contextually
- **store.go**: Mode is already persisted (parseMode handles it)
close_reason: Design complete. Key decisions: (1) Mode toggle via Tab in new-stream overlay + m key to switch mid-stream. (2) Pause check lives in loop.go at two points: override TransitionMode on convergence, and pause after every iteration in pairing mode. (3) TUI shows [Pair] badge on dashboard, workflow is s-to-iterate with g-for-guidance between iterations. (4) No inline prompt editing in v1 — guidance mechanism covers the use case. See --design field for full writeup.

title: Design git branch strategy and working directory isolation
description: Two related decisions needed before coding. (1) Git branch: The coding phase makes commits and runs autosquash rebases. On what branch? Options: stream creates a new branch from HEAD, user specifies a branch, or work on current branch. Autosquash needs a known base commit — the stream probably needs to record a BaseSHA at creation time. (2) Working directory: Does the stream create a git worktree or work in-place? In-place is simpler but blocks the user from using the repo during a run. Affects the CLI runtime's WorkDir config. Both decisions affect multi-stream later but need answering for single-stream v1.
notes: Decisions: (1) Stream creates dedicated branch streams/<stream-id> from HEAD, records BaseSHA for autosquash rebase target. (2) Git worktree at .streams/worktrees/<stream-id>. Stream struct adds BaseSHA, Branch, WorkTree fields. Worktree created via git worktree add, cleaned up via git worktree remove on stream completion.

title: Update default plan-implement template with planning guidelines
description:
Update the default plan-implement.tmpl to include these planning guidelines (from user's global CLAUDE.md):

- Start with one simple case end-to-end. Don't implement all variations at once. Pick the simplest case and wire it through every layer, then expand.
- Order changes chronologically. Each step should follow the order a human would think about the feature.
- Keep commits small. Each commit is one logical step.
- Batch remaining work by implementation similarity. After the first case works end-to-end, group remaining items by what they have in common.
- Write tests alongside changes. Each step includes the tests relevant to that step's changes.

These guidelines should NOT be included (they conflict with streams' orchestration):
- 'Store plans in docs/plans/' — streams writes plan.md in the working directory
- 'Name the plan using format [timestamp]-[git branch]' — streams uses plan.md
- 'Present three options after planning' — streams is automated, no human prompt
- 'Create beads issues for plan steps' — the decompose phase handles this separately

title: Design quality gate evaluation
description: The Gate interface exists (gates.go) but is never called and has no implementations. Questions to resolve: (1) How are gates parsed from review text? The architecture says 'parsed from review output' but the prompts don't ask the reviewer to structure output for gate extraction. (2) Should gates be hard-coded checklist items or LLM-evaluated? (3) Do gate failures block convergence or are they advisory? (4) The 4 default gates (pattern conformance, simplicity, readability, hindsight) — are these separate LLM calls, string-matching on review text, or something else? Propose a concrete design.
close_reason: Gates as spec'd duplicate the review agent's work. The review already evaluates pattern conformance, simplicity, readability, and correctness, and files issues for failures. Gates would just be a lossy summary of that. The Gate interface stays unimplemented until a concrete differentiating use case emerges (e.g., automated/deterministic checks like test pass or linter clean, or user-defined custom criteria).

title: Implement loop engine
description: Create internal/loop/loop.go. Main function: Run(ctx context.Context, s *stream.Stream, phase MacroPhase, rt runtime.Runtime, beads BeadsQuerier) — no return value, outcome reflected in stream state (StatusPaused+Converged, StatusPaused+LastError, or StatusStopped). Loop flow per iteration: (1) check ctx.Done, (2) fetch ordered steps via beads.FetchOrderedSteps, (3) StepImplement — count open children before, call rt.Run with implement prompt, count after, check ErrNoProgress on iteration>0, (4) StepAutosquash — call phase.BeforeReview, (5) StepReview — call rt.Run with review prompt, count open children after, (6) convergence check via phase.IsConverged, (7) StepCheckpoint — build Snapshot and append to stream. On convergence set Converged+StatusPaused. Check ctx.Err() after each rt.Run to distinguish cancellation from runtime errors. Include orchestratorRules constant (CLAUDE.md override block from ARCHITECTURE.md). Helpers: buildRequest (constructs runtime.Request with allowedTools, appendSystemPrompt=orchestratorRules, permissionMode=dontAsk), classifyError (checks for 'budget' keyword), recordError (sets LastError, StatusPaused, appends error snapshot, logs via slog).

close_reason: Added header row, thin separator, left-border accent (▎) for selected rows, dynamic name width (capped at 40), worktree info on secondary line. Visual-width padding for ANSI-styled status column.

title: Mid-flight guidance cancellation
description: Currently guidance submitted while the implement step is running sits in the queue until StepGuidance at the end of the iteration. ARCHITECTURE.md documents a future enhancement: cancel the running CLI process via context cancellation and restart the iteration with guidance injected, giving the user immediate steering. Discuss: is the latency of waiting for the current iteration acceptable, or is this worth the complexity?

title: Refine color palette
description: Replace harsh 256-color values with refined ones. See docs/plans/vectorized-singing-blossom.md item 2. Primary→33, Success→34, Error→160, Warning→214, add colorSubtle (236) and colorHighlight (39). Add Faint(true) for metadata, background colors for selected rows.
close_reason: Refined all 4 base colors to 256-color values, added colorSubtle/colorHighlight/metadataStyle, added background on selected rows

title: Record project direction: design-first, vertical slices
description: Prototype (streams-t2v) and debugging (streams-291) work was scrapped. New approach: iterate on architecture and design more thoroughly, then build features as vertical slices (end-to-end through all layers). Record this decision so future sessions have context.
close_reason: Decision recorded: scrapped prototype, taking design-first approach with vertical slices
